rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or declared as helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions, using int, real, or bool as appropriate.
Avoid placing invalid guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex scope.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations.
Prefer assign for implementation-level output bindings over guarantee statements in implementation scopes.
Validate that binary expressions in AGREE have matching left and right side types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Helper declarations (eq) must have consistent types between left-hand side and right-hand side expressions.
Package-qualified identifiers in AGREE must match configured AADL dependencies.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers in AGREE expressions; remove invalid clauses instead of adding architecture.
Do not mix incompatible types in binary expressions (e.g., int and real) without explicit conversion.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output features.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope placement
